content:
as getting caught in a thorny bush and nearly falling into a ditch, her perseverance mirrors the
legendary rebirth of the phoenix. Her struggle with the bush is particularly symbolic: “I in the
thorny bush,
” she said.
“Thorns, you doing your appointed work. Never want to let folks pass, no
sir” (Welty, 1941, p. 2)【20】. Here, the thorns represent the hardships in her life, but she
acknowledges them as part of her journey, reinforcing her resilience.
The Worn Path as a Symbol of Sacrifice
The path Phoenix takes is another central symbol in the story. It represents both her love for her
grandson and the sacrifices she endures to care for him. The path is not just a literal route; it is
a metaphor for her repeated struggles. The journey is long and difficult, yet she has traveled it
many times before, underscoring the routine nature of her sacrifice. This is emphasized when
the nurse recognizes her immediately: “Oh, that’s just old Aunt Phoenix,
